text.title: Email Lifecycle Architecture Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Email Lifecycle Architecture application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Map every email to one of six stages: acquisition, activation, engagement, retention, resurrection, churn. Unmapped emails = noise.
rule.rationale: Without stage mapping, programs cannibalize each other; explicit map enables conflict resolution.

rule.statement: Global frequency cap per subscriber: max N emails / week (typically 3-5). Cap is enforced ACROSS programs, not per-program.
rule.rationale: Per-program caps still over-mail; global cap is the only one that respects subscriber inbox.

rule.statement: Suppression list is global. A user who opts out of marketing is suppressed from ALL marketing programs, not just the one they unsubscribed from.
rule.rationale: Per-program suppression is a GDPR + CAN-SPAM risk; global suppression is the only safe shape.

rule.statement: Every subscriber has a consent record: opt-in date, source (form, import, purchase), legal basis (consent, contract, legitimate interest).
rule.rationale: GDPR Art. 7: must demonstrate consent; missing record = unprovable + treat as no-consent.

rule.statement: Quarterly audit: bounce rate, spam complaint rate, unsubscribe rate per program. Programs above threshold (e.g., spam complaint >0.1%) pause until fixed.
rule.rationale: Deliverability tanks 60-90 days after threshold breach; quarterly audit catches drift.
